library(BayesFactor)
Loading required package: coda
Loading required package: Matrix

Attaching package: ‘Matrix’

The following objects are masked from ‘package:tidyr’:

    expand, pack, unpack

************
Welcome to BayesFactor 0.9.12-4.7. If you have questions, please contact Richard Morey (richarddmorey@gmail.com).

Type BFManual() to open the manual.

library(BayesianId)
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% rename(y = body_mass, x1 = flipper_len, x2 = bill_len, f1 = island, f2 = sex)
test_data = test_data |> dplyr::mutate(y_bin = test_data$f2, y_cens = ifelse(y > 5000, 5000, y), censoring = (y > 5000))

levels(test_data$f1) |> print()
[1] "Biscoe"    "Dream"     "Torgersen"
levels(test_data$f2) |> print()
[1] "female" "male"  
nrow(test_data) |> print()
[1] 333
tic = Sys.time()
bar = breg(y ~ f1, 
           data = test_data,
           family = "normal_linear",
           #family = "right_censored_linear",
           #family = "bernoulli_logistic",
           chains = 2,
           iter = 10000
           )
toc = Sys.time()
print(toc - tic)
Time difference of 0.5949421 secs
plot(bar)

emmeans(bar, "f1") |> summary()
Warning: argument is not numeric or logical: returning NA
 f1        emmean   SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
 Biscoe      4717 49.8 Inf      4619      4814
 Dream       3723 56.8 Inf      3611      3834
 Torgersen   3717 91.4 Inf      3538      3897

Confidence level used: 0.95 
library(emmeans)
emmeans(bar, "f1") |> summary()
Warning: argument is not numeric or logical: returning NANOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
 f1        emmean   SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
 Biscoe      4710 41.9 Inf      4628      4793
 Dream       3719 47.1 Inf      3627      3811
 Torgersen   3722 75.9 Inf      3573      3870

Results are averaged over the levels of: f2 
Confidence level used: 0.95 
data("Loblolly")
Loblolly = as.data.frame(Loblolly)
Loblolly$Seed = factor(Loblolly$Seed)
Loblolly[1:15,]
foo = parse_formula(height ~ age + (age | Seed))
foo
foo$random
bar = paste(foo$lhs, "~", paste(foo$random$Seed, collapse = " + ")) |> formula()
bar
Xg_list = list()
for(i in 1:length(levels(Loblolly$Seed))){
  Xg_list[[i]] = model.matrix(bar, data = Loblolly |> subset(Seed == levels(Loblolly$Seed)[[i]]))
}
Xg = Matrix::bdiag(Xg_list)
Xg
glmm_matrix = function(formula, data){
  formula_info = parse_formula(formula)
  gfactor = names(formula_info$random)[1]
  gef_formula = paste(foo$lhs, "~", paste(formula_info$random[[gfactor]], collapse = " + ")) |> formula()
  
  Xg_list = list()
  for(i in 1:length(levels(data[,gfactor]))){
    data_subset = data[data[,gfactor] == levels(data[,gfactor])[[i]],]
    Xg_list[[i]] = model.matrix(gef_formula, data = data_subset)
  }
  Xg = bdiag(Xg_list)
  return(Xg)
}
glmm_matrix(height ~ age + (age | Seed), data = Loblolly)
